arguments.command: which llvm-symbolizer addr2line llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; apt list --installed 2>/dev/null | grep -i llvm | head -5

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to symbolicate the .NET runtime frames in the backtrace. The tombstone shows frames from `libcoreclr.so` with BuildId `4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801`. 

To symbolicate these, I need to:
1. Find the debug symbols for this specific build of `libcoreclr.so`
2. Use a tool like `llvm-symbolizer` or `addr2line` to resolve the addresses to function names

The BuildId is `4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801`. This is a .NET runtime library. Microsoft publishes symbol files for .NET runtime builds.


Microsoft hosts a symbol server at `https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols` where I can look up symbols using the BuildId. For ELF files on Linux/Android, the symbol server uses a specific URL format with the BuildId embedded in the path to retrieve the debug information.

I have the BuildId from the tombstone (`4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801`), and I'm working through the correct URL pattern to construct the request—it looks like the format should include the filename and BuildId in a specific order, possibly with the BuildId lowercase and without hyphens.

Let me try downloading the debug symbols using one of these URL patterns, then I can use tools like llvm-symbolizer or addr2line to resolve the stack trace addresses back to function names and line numbers.

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command:
# Try downloading the debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server
# BuildId: 4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801
BUILDID="4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801"
mkdir -p /tmp/sv-e71dcc0eef86490b89a7bdc722e84c5f/symbols

# Try the ELF debug info pattern
curl -sSL -o /tmp/sv-e71dcc0eef86490b89a7bdc722e84c5f/symbols/libcoreclr.so.dbg \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug" \
  -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n" 2>&1 | tail -1

# Also try the binary with symbols
curl -sSL -o /tmp/sv-e71dcc0eef86490b89a7bdc722e84c5f/symbols/libcoreclr.so \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libcoreclr.so/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/libcoreclr.so" \
  -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n" 2>&1 | tail -1
arguments.description: Download CoreCLR debug symbols from MS symbol server

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Download CoreCLR debug symbols from MS symbol server

data.reasoningText:
I have `addr2line` available. Now I need to get the debug symbols for `libcoreclr.so` with BuildId `4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801`.

Let me try downloading from the Microsoft symbol server. The typical URL patterns are:

1. For the binary itself: `https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libcoreclr.so/elf-buildid-<buildid>/libcoreclr.so`
2. For debug info: `https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-<buildid>/_.debug`


Let me check if `llvm-symbolizer` is available as an alternative, then attempt to download the symbols using the Microsoft symbol server or the dotnet symbol tool.

mkdir -p /tmp/sv-e71dcc0eef86490b89a7bdc722e84c5f/symbols

# Try the ELF debug info pattern
curl -sSL -o /tmp/sv-e71dcc0eef86490b89a7bdc722e84c5f/symbols/libcoreclr.so.dbg \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug" \
  -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n" 2>&1 | tail -1

# Also try the binary with symbols
curl -sSL -o /tmp/sv-e71dcc0eef86490b89a7bdc722e84c5f/symbols/libcoreclr.so \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libcoreclr.so/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/libcoreclr.so" \
  -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n" 2>&1 | tail -1
toolArgs.description: Download CoreCLR debug symbols from MS symbol server
